title: Evidence Boundaries and Adoption Limits
subtitle: What current evidence supports, what remains open, and what resistance still needs documentation
summary: Evidence-boundary entry for the Sutton SignWriting Evidence and Adoption series.
synopsis: This entry states the limits of current SignWriting evidence as a first-class part of the empirical series. It distinguishes documented use from outcome claims, names underdocumented adoption limits and negative cases, and keeps Brazil and South Korea from being overgeneralized.

slides.title: Why Boundaries Matter
slides.summary: Evidence boundaries make the adoption case more credible by separating documented use from unproven claims.

image_prompts.concrete: Scholarly slide showing verified evidence inside a clear boundary and open questions outside it
image_prompts.abstract: A bright evidence field surrounded by a careful boundary line

bullets: Country anchors should not be overgeneralized
Open questions belong in the record
slides.speech: Evidence boundaries are not a retreat from the adoption case. They make the case stronger. The record can show real use while also naming what has not yet been proven about scale, outcomes, resistance, and community preference.
slides.notes: Make this sound like research discipline, not caution for its own sake.

slides.title: What Evidence Supports
slides.summary: Current evidence supports existence, documented anchors, technical maturity, and research relevance.

image_prompts.concrete: Four-part evidence slide labeled existence, anchors, technical maturity, and research relevance
image_prompts.abstract: Four verified blocks supporting a careful evidence claim

bullets: South Korea provides current institutional evidence
Technical and scholarly records are substantial
slides.speech: The current evidence supports several strong claims. SignWriting is real in practice. Brazil provides empirical depth. South Korea provides current institutional evidence. The technical and scholarly record is substantial enough to support serious research and stewardship.

slides.title: What Remains Open
slides.summary: The evidence does not yet provide a complete global map, broad literacy outcomes, or full documentation of adoption resistance.

image_prompts.concrete: Research agenda slide showing open boxes for global mapping, literacy outcomes, community preference, and adoption resistance
image_prompts.abstract: Open evidence boxes marked for future documentation

bullets: Community desire cannot be assumed everywhere
Negative and dormant cases need documentation
slides.speech: Several questions remain open. The record is not a complete global map. Large-scale literacy outcome evidence remains limited. Community desire cannot be assumed from a few strong cases. Adoption limits, resistance, and dormant programs need better documentation.
slides.notes: Name gaps plainly without making the whole project sound weak.

slides.title: Responsible Use
slides.summary: Responsible readers can cite strong anchors while keeping broader adoption and outcome claims appropriately bounded.

image_prompts.concrete: Citation-oriented slide showing strong evidence anchors connected to bounded responsible claims
image_prompts.abstract: A citation path passing through a clear evidence filter

bullets: Use open questions to guide research
slides.speech: The practical result is a stronger citation posture. Readers can cite documented anchors directly, distinguish existence from outcomes, keep country claims specific, and use open questions as a research agenda rather than as a reason to dismiss the system.
slides.notes: This is the usable takeaway for scholars and reviewers.
